title: search — trait composition
description: Owns `HasSearchable` — the opt-in trait models pull in alongside their #[Searchable] attribute. Wraps laravel/scout's own `Searchable` trait and layers our conventions (tenant scope on push, permission-scoped toSearchableArray, restricted-tier field filtering, alias-aware routing). Also owns the trait composition on our own SearchIndex + SearchSyncJob + SearchSynonym + SearchSavedQuery + SearchAnalyticsEvent models.

owned.description: Opt-in trait for models registering with the search pipeline. Wraps scout's `Searchable` + adds our defaults: tenant-scoped push, permission-scoped `toSearchableArray()`, restricted-tier field filtering (never indexes those columns), alias-aware routing (`Model::search()` hits `{index}_live`), and integration with the SearchIndex registry.
usedBy: any domain module opting a model into search (athletes, teams, invitations, newsletter subscribers, courses, ...)
notes: Also registers a `resolveSearchableFields()` static method the compiler calls to merge attribute discovery with an optional dynamic escape hatch.
Composes with domain traits — BelongsToTenant + HasUlids + HasActivityLog + HasAudit as needed by the model.
The model may still override scout's `toSearchableArray()` per its contract for finer control; our trait supplies a sensible default from #[SearchField] declarations.
Adds a `search()` static (delegates to scout's) + a `searchableAs()` implementation returning the live alias name.
Automatic push on `saved` + purge on `deleted` via scout's observers — no manual dispatching needed.

foundation.reason: Free-form platform notes on the operation record + saved query.
Platform-seeded synonym rows carry is_system=true — they can be disabled but not deleted by tenants.

activity.reason: Every mutation writes a product-feed row. Analytics events do NOT dual-write (query volume would drown the feed).

audit.reason: Every index registration / unregistration + sync-job transition writes a compliance-grade audit row. Synonym / saved-query / analytics do NOT audit (product concerns, not compliance).

laravel_scout.reason: The raw Scout trait we compose on. Applied to CONSUMER models (via our HasSearchable), NOT to search's own models.

search_index.notes: Full lifecycle stack — dual-write to both activity and audit on registration / unregistration / alias swap.

search_synonym.notes: HasSystemFlag marks platform-seeded rows. HasActivityLog covers CRUD (create / update / disable / delete) so tenant admins see 'Alice added synonym flu ↔ influenza'. No HasAudit — synonym mutations are tenant business decisions, not compliance material.

search_saved_query.notes: Same rationale as SearchSynonym on the auditing decision.

search_analytics_event.notes: Lightweight, append-only. NO SoftDeletes (hard-pruned on retention). NO HasActivityLog (would drown the feed — every query fires one). NO HasAudit (query volume is telemetry, not evidence).
